items.decision_rationale: Standard new referral for SLP articulation evaluation. All core intake fields present: child name, DOB, parent contact information, discipline, clinical concern, and insurance details. Referral source is established pediatrician. Patient preference for Tuesday/Thursday after-school availability noted for scheduling. No safety concerns, urgency signals, or same-day operational needs. Classify as P2 routine intake workflow.

args.reason: Possible child-safety disclosure in inbound message; requires same-hour clinical review.

args.notes: Inbound message contains possible safeguarding concern. Do not provide investigative advice to the family; clinical lead to review and determine mandated-reporter steps.
args.title: Same-hour safeguarding review for Leo

args.body: Thank you for reaching out about your child. A member of our clinical team will follow up with you directly. We're here to help and will be in touch shortly.

items.recommended_next_action: Clinical lead to review the escalation this hour and determine mandated-reporter obligations before any routine intake or scheduling.
items.draft_reply: Thank you for reaching out about your child. A member of our clinical team will follow up with you directly. We're here to help and will be in touch shortly.

escalation.reason: Possible child-safety disclosure; safeguarding policy requires immediate clinical escalation.

items.decision_rationale: Safeguarding signal detected (possible harm or unsafe caregiving). Per policy this is forced to P0 with immediate clinical escalation, regardless of the requested service. Model note: Safeguarding signal triggered by parental disclosure that child 'has been more clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends.' This language suggests potential physical abuse or rough/violent caregiving. Despite the parent framing this as a side comment while requesting speech therapy, the substance indicates a child safety concern requiring immediate mandated-reporter review and assessment per Cedar Kids' protocols. Escalated to P0 for same-hour human review by clinical leadership and compliance. Speech therapy intake is secondary to safety assessment.

args.notes: Parent asked a developmental question. Per policy, do not answer clinically by message; offer a screening/evaluation pathway.
args.title: Route clinical question to screening for Ava

args.body: Thank you for your question. Every child develops at their own pace, and the best way to get a clear answer is a short screening with one of our speech-language pathologists. We can't give clinical advice by message, but we'd be glad to set up an evaluation so a clinician can take a look.

items.recommended_next_action: Intake to offer a screening/evaluation appointment; a clinician (not the front desk) addresses the developmental question.
items.draft_reply: Thank you for your question. Every child develops at their own pace, and the best way to get a clear answer is a short screening with one of our speech-language pathologists. We can't give clinical advice by message, but we'd be glad to set up an evaluation so a clinician can take a look.
items.decision_rationale: Parent is seeking clinical guidance about typical development of R sounds in a 4-year-old, not requesting immediate scheduling or reporting a safety concern. This is a straightforward clinical question appropriate for P2 triage. An SLP can provide developmentally informed guidance during normal intake/consultation workflow. Parent explicitly states they would appreciate advice before booking, indicating this is information-gathering rather than urgent. No safety signals present.

args.title: Same-day reschedule for Noah Patel

args.body: Thanks for letting us know, and we hope your child feels better soon. We've flagged today's appointment for our front desk to reschedule, and someone will reach out shortly with options.

items.recommended_next_action: Front desk to call the family today to confirm cancellation and offer a makeup evaluation slot.
items.draft_reply: Thanks for letting us know, and we hope your child feels better soon. We've flagged today's appointment for our front desk to reschedule, and someone will reach out shortly with options.

escalation.reason: Same-day reschedule requires prompt front-desk action today.

items.decision_rationale: Same-day cancellation of an existing OT appointment (3pm today). Parent explicitly requests reschedule. This is a P1 operational issue requiring prompt staff action to modify the schedule and contact parent with alternatives. The vomiting is a situational reason for the cancellation, not a safeguarding concern. Multiple exclamation points reflect parent stress but do not escalate urgency beyond the concrete same-day scheduling need.
